content:
Difference between endonuclease and exonuclease activity?
Are MMR enzymes always a part of the DNAP?
When, specifically does it choose to do endonuclease cleaving at the phosphodiesters vs exonuclease single bp?
How does UV Light, high energy radition, X-Rays, chemicals get into the body to modify nucleic acids?
What does oxidation, deamination, and alkylation of bases look like?
How does the base excision repair system rank?
Why is it called DNA glycosylase enzyme?
So you can divide the specific DNA repair pathways based on if damage was done to the double helix or not?
So the order is recognizing non double helix affected nucleotide damage, then a glycosylase will excise, then a endonuclease will cleave... so does the phosphodiester sugar backbone stay in tact when the glycosylase excises?
It seems the standard repair mecahnism once you have a clean slate is to put back on a DNAP and then a DNA ligase to rejoin the strands to seal nicks?
This is all in comparison to the nucleotide excision repair system (NER)?
Why are thymine dimers bad?
That distorts the double helix, thymine dimers?
Sometimes the chromsome itself, which Im not sure how do you define a chromsome?
Anyway, sometimes the chromosome itself has a double stranded break, what might cause a double stranded break, the same factors as before?
